meta.title: Interactive graph: collapsible bubbles and a flowing spine
meta.summary: A UI rework of the report. Every node became a collapsible circular bubble that morphs open, the spine flows in the node's own colour on hover, and the report grew a per-conversation history with a master index.

nodes.title: Make the report's nodes interactive bubbles
nodes.summary: Rework the graph so nodes are circular bubbles with big icons that expand on click, with hover animations, a priority staircase, and connecting narration between steps.
nodes.rationale: A wall of open cards is hard to scan. Folding each step into a labelled bubble turns the report into a navigable map you choose to open.
nodes.transition: The first move was to decide what a folded node should look like.

nodes.title: Collapse each node into a morphing graph bubble
nodes.summary: Each node starts as a big kind-coloured icon face with its title beside it, and clicking morphs it into the full card.
nodes.rationale: Animating border-radius, fill and padding on a single element (rather than swapping two elements) keeps the open and closed states reading as one object resizing, with no layout jump.
nodes.transition: Showing the bubble raised the question of how it sits on the spine.

materials.title: Using CSS transitions (MDN)
materials.note: The transition model behind the morph from bubble to card.

nodes.title: One marker, drawn in front of the lines
nodes.summary: The kind icon is the only marker in both states; the separate spine bead was removed, and the bubble is lifted above the connecting lines.
nodes.rationale: Two circles (a bead plus a badge) read as clutter and an accidental duplicate when a card opened. Collapsing to one marker, with the spine tucked behind it via z-index, makes the graph legible.
nodes.transition: With the shape settled, the connecting lines could be brought to life.

materials.title: CSS stacking and z-index (MDN)
materials.note: Why the bubble needs its own stacking context to sit above the pseudo-element lines.

nodes.title: Restyle the collapsed state in render.py
nodes.summary: Edited the renderer's CSS so the collapsed card sheds its chrome and the icon swells to a 58px face.
nodes.rationale: All of the report's style lives inline in render.py, so the bubble look is a focused set of edits to the collapsed-state rules.
nodes.transition: Hovering then needed to feel alive, not static.

nodes.title: Light the spine with a flowing current on hover
nodes.summary: Hovering a bubble lights its connector and spine segment in the bubble's own colour, with a brighter band flowing toward the next node.
nodes.rationale: A moving gradient reads as live current passing from one step to the next, reinforcing that the graph is a story you follow top to bottom.
nodes.transition: Finally, a single repo holds many conversations, so reports needed history.

materials.title: Using CSS animations (MDN)
materials.note: The keyframe + background-position technique behind the flowing band.

nodes.title: Give each conversation its own report, linked from a master index
nodes.summary: Reports are written per conversation under .goldy/reports/, and a master index page links to every one, newest first.
nodes.rationale: A repo accumulates many sessions. Overwriting one file would lose the history, so each session is its own report and a manifest (index.json) drives a hub page that ties them together.
nodes.transition: Taken together these changes make the report friendlier to navigate.

nodes.title: In summary: a friendlier, navigable report
nodes.summary:
The rework left the report easier to read and to keep:

- **Folded bubbles** that morph open, so you scan first and dive in on demand.
- **A living spine** that flows in each step's colour on hover.
- **A priority staircase** and **story bridges** that show the shape and the narrative at a glance.
- **Per-conversation history** with a master index, so a repo's sessions accumulate instead of overwriting.
nodes.rationale: Each change serves the same goal: make a session's reasoning quick to navigate and durable to revisit.
